The most substantive technical content came from shawmakesmagic, who mentioned they are currently using Qwen models with optimizations and are developing a fine-tuning pipeline to improve model performance for action calling and planning. The rest of the conversation consisted primarily of greetings, self-promotional messages about job opportunities and investment inquiries, and moderation activity. faqs.question: What models are currently being used and what are the plans for improvement?

categories.summary: The channel featured a significant technical post about a novel escrow protocol designed specifically for AI agents deployed on Base Mainnet. The protocol implements a 'Pay or Burn' mechanism that eliminates human arbitration bottlenecks by using game theory - if agents cannot agree on task outcomes, escrowed funds are burned, destroying the expected value for malicious actors. Key architectural decisions include 100% pull-payment design for protection against reverting receiver contracts and zero UI approach with only raw smart contracts and SDKs in Python/C++. The creator is seeking architectural feedback from the community. A separate technical discussion involved Neynar API credit consumption issues, where a user reported unexpectedly high usage (300 credits/hour vs expected 4/hour) on v2/farcaster/notifications endpoint despite overriding the default 120-second polling interval. A community member suggested webhooks as a potential solution to reduce credit consumption. The channel also saw job postings for blockchain gaming positions and AI/ML engineering services.

faqs.question: Is there any documentation on Neynar credits consumption? My agent is consuming around 300 credits per hour on v2/farcaster/notifications, but should only be doing 4 per hour even after overriding the default 120 second call.
faqs.answeredBy: odilitime suggested webhooks could reduce consumption

helpInteractions.context: High Neynar API credit consumption (300/hour instead of expected 4/hour) on notifications endpoint
helpInteractions.resolution: Suggested using webhooks as an alternative to reduce credit usage

actionItems.description: Review and provide architectural critiques for the AI agent escrow protocol with Pay or Burn mechanism on Base Mainnet
Provide clearer documentation on Neynar credits consumption and billing for API endpoints
Investigate webhook implementation to reduce Neynar API credit consumption

categories.summary: The discussion centered on two main topics: a partnership announcement and V3 release timing. ivgamer3467 provided details about a deal between Secure Blockchain Development Corp. and the Eliza Foundation to commercialize an Agentic SME solution using the ElizaOS framework. The partnership timeline includes a 30-day period for v1 development followed by 90 days for full deployment. This represents a significant commercial application of the ElizaOS framework for small and medium enterprises. The discussion also touched on V3 release expectations, with lalanotia noting it had been 2 weeks since Shaw indicated an imminent release. There was some skepticism from ame05230523 regarding the partnership details, but ivgamer3467 clarified it involves a Canadian listed entity with outlined timelines. A minor moderation inquiry about unbanning a user named 'arisae' was addressed by odilitime, who confirmed no user by that name was found in the ban list.

faqs.question: When will the V3 be released?
Is anyone with the name arisae banned?

helpInteractions.context: Request to unban user named arisae
helpInteractions.resolution: Checked ban list and confirmed no user with that name was found banned

actionItems.description: Complete v1 of Agentic SME solution within 30-day timeline for Secure Blockchain Development Corp partnership
Full deployment of Agentic SME solution using ElizaOS framework within 90 days following v1

categories.summary: The main discussion centered on elizaOS v2.0.0 release announcement and token economics clarification. A new version (v2.0.0) was released, with users inquiring about release notes and features. The most significant technical discussion involved mbat8088 asking detailed questions about $elizaOS token utility and economics. Key questions covered: legal structure relationship between Milady and elizaOS, buyback mechanisms from product profits, potential Milady token plans, buyback percentage targets, and governance structure. odilitime provided authoritative answers clarifying that elizaOS did not launch Milady but built on their stack using fees, Milady has separate cloud infrastructure for its own buybacks, no additional tokens planned except possibly Babylon in distant future, no formal buyback percentage set as focus remains on achieving profitability first, and no formal governance structure currently exists. The discussion revealed the project is in early stages focusing on product delivery (elizaOS framework, Milady app, Eliza Cloud) before finalizing tokenomics details. Two developers (keil0780 and rsn6958) posted availability for collaboration on AI and full-stack projects. A scammer was called out by mbat8088. Reference was made to v3 launch being close and previous post by Odi in another channel from May 3rd.

faqs.question: What is the $elizaOS token utility?
What's new in v2? Cannot find release notes
Are Milady and elizaOS the same legal entity?
Does product profits buyback include Milady revenue or only Eliza Cloud revenue?
Is there a separate Milady token planned?
Is there a specific buyback percentage target?
Will buyback percentage be set through formal governance or team discretion?

helpInteractions.context: mbat8088 asked about elizaOS token utility and was directed to information
helpInteractions.resolution: Pointed to post from Odi in another channel from May 3rd

helpInteractions.context: Detailed questions about token economics, legal structure, buybacks, and governance
helpInteractions.resolution: Provided comprehensive answers clarifying Milady relationship, no new tokens planned, no formal buyback percentage yet, and no formal governance structure

actionItems.description: Publish v2.0.0 release notes with feature details
Formalize and document token buyback percentage and governance mechanisms
Focus on achieving profitability with product stack before finalizing tokenomics

Two full-stack developers (keil0780 and trace.g) posted detailed introductions offering their services. keil0780 specializes in React, Node.js, Python, OpenAI API, and PyTorch, focusing on AI-integrated applications and workflow automation. trace.g positions themselves as an AI + Full Stack Engineer specializing in LLM systems, agents, RAG, multimodal AI, and automation, emphasizing their ability to transition projects from manual workflows and prototypes to production-ready AI systems with clean orchestration and scalable backend flows. Both developers are actively seeking freelance, contract, or collaboration opportunities. No technical problems were solved, no specific implementations were discussed, and no concrete decisions were made during this segment. The conversation represents typical community networking and service offerings rather than active development discussion.

faqs.question: What is everyone here working on?

categories.summary: The discussion centered on significant technical achievements with ElizaOS. zadayos successfully booted ElizaOS Debian RISC-V 64-bit ISO on QEMU chip emulator in 35 seconds, demonstrating cross-architecture compatibility. shawmakesmagic confirmed Eliza v3 is running on elizaOS (Linux) on an emulated RISC-V chip called 'eliza e1', representing a major milestone in hardware abstraction. When asked about custom chip development, the question about a physical Eliza E1 chip remained unanswered. On the business side, shawmakesmagic clarified that the previously mentioned Babylon airdrop for elizaos holders will not happen due to budget constraints, with the team now focusing exclusively on elizaOS development. This strategic pivot was strongly supported by zadayos, who advocated for single-token focus similar to ETH/SOL utility models, leaving ecosystem token creation to the market. Regarding Milady token buyback, odilitime confirmed it's the BNB version. janabr8973 announced a new DeFi plugin for Eliza on Base/Aave, offering real-time solvency scoring and flash-crash simulations via NPM package @centinel-risk-protocol/eliza-plugin-centinel, seeking community testing and feedback.

faqs.question: Do you intend to make your own chip (Eliza E1) for Elizaos in the future?
Which milady token buyback with milady app revenue? It's on BNB or on Solana?
Is the CA on coingecko correct for bnb/base/eth?
Any update for degenai?
I remember an airdrop to elizaos holders was mentioned long ago did it happen?

helpInteractions.context: Confusion about which Milady token (BNB or Solana) is used for buyback with Milady app revenue
helpInteractions.resolution: Confirmed it's the BNB version of the token

actionItems.description: Test and provide feedback on Centinel Risk Protocol Eliza plugin for DeFi agents on Base/Aave with real-time solvency scoring and flash-crash simulations
Focus exclusively on elizaOS development and create multiple utility token uses similar to ETH or SOL model
